page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u2a-c1m4.2xlarge is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine instance running on the KVM hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC processor operating at a clock speed of 2.25 GHz. The instance is configured with 1.0 thread per core, ensuring dedicated CPU allocation without hyperthreading. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core.

This instance does not include local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met through attached network volumes. It offers a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps and does not include any complimentary public IPv4 addresses. There are no integrated GPU accelerators. The lack of local storage and hardware acceleration represents a tradeoff, positioning this instance for workloads that rely on network-attached storage and standard CPU processing.

With its 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the ecs.u2a-c1m4.2xlarge is classified as a general-purpose instance. It is designed for resource-balanced workloads such as mid-range web servers, application servers, containerized microservices, and development environments. The dedicated AMD EPYC cores provide predictable compute performance, making it a qualitatively cost-efficient option for steady-state workloads that do not require burstable CPU credits or high-performance local disk I/O.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u2a-c1m4.2xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance built on the KVM hypervisor and powered by an x86_64 AMD EPYC processor. It features 8 dedicated physical cores with a clock speed of 2.25 GHz and a single thread per core configuration, ensuring dedicated CPU allocation. The instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, requiring network-attached storage for data persistence. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 3 Gbps. This resource profile makes the instance suitable for balanced workloads such as web serving, application hosting, and containerized deployments that require predictable CPU performance without the need for high-speed local storage.

tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual instance featuring dedicated AMD EPYC cores and a stable resource-to-memory ratio for standard workloads.
